form.title: Regulatory transaction template: Regulatory enrolment process (REP) for medical devices
refresh.file.input: Refresh this page to clear form
warning.text:
This template is used to file medical device licence application to Health Canada.


The web-based Extensible Markup Language regulatory transaction file (REP RT XML) must accompany every medical device transaction sent through the electronic submissions gateway (ESG).


Ensure that all fields in this template are completed in the sequential order. The selections made in certain fields may affect the visibility and availability of other fields and sections throughout the form.
transaction.description.default.option: First, select a regulatory activity type.
device.name: Name of device, as it appears on the label
licence.name: Proposed licence name
include.ddt: Include the device details excel file with the corresponding 'device name change' and/or 'addition/deletion' sheet completed with this submission.
are.fees: Are new fees or revised fees associated with this transaction?
completed.fee.required: The applicable fees will be invoiced; however, a completed fee form is still required.
error.mgs.dossier.id: Invalid entry - must be lowercase "m" followed by 6 digits
error.mgs.licence.number: Invalid entry - must be 6 digits - Add leading 0 if less than 6 digits
msg.err.parse.fail: Error: there was an error parsing the file.
msg.err.file.load: There was a problem loading the file.
msg.err.file.type: An invalid file type was selected.
msg.err.form.type: The wrong form type was selected for this form.
msg.err.checksum: Error: there was an error parsing the file.
instruction.help: Instructions for completing sections on form
section.show.option: [ This section may not be visible. ]
help.load.file.02: When continuing with a working copy, the format of the file to load should be .hcsc.
help.load.file.03: When viewing/updating a final XML file, the format of the file to load should be .xml.
help.transaction.01: The company identifier must be obtained from the final company XML file, issued by Health Canada.
help.transaction.02: The dossier identifier can be obtained from the final dossier XML file or other Health Canada issued document (for example m######).
help.dossierId.01: The dossier identifier is a code created by Health Canada to uniquely identify the dossier.  If a dossier ID has not yet been assigned, manufacturers must request for one using the medical device dossier ID request form.
help.dossierId.02: The format of a dossier identifier is a lowercase "m" followed by six digits (for example m######).
help.contactId: All regulatory correspondence will be sent to this contact, but the licence will be issued to the manufacturer. A medical device licence application can be submitted by a third party.
help.transactionDesc1: Unsolicited information includes any information submitted during the review process that was not requested, as well as updates to the regulatory contact.
help.transactionDesc2: A "Response to reconsideration information request" includes any information submitted in response to requests made during the reconsideration process.
help.transactionDesc3: The "Date of request" refers to the date of the letter issued by Health Canada that this transaction is in response to.
help.deviceClass.02: Guidance Document - Guidance on the Risk-based Classification System for Non-In Vitro Diagnostic Devices (non-IVDDs)
help.application.number: This number is assigned by Health Canada for each application/transaction received.
help.device.name: Only one name should be entered in this field. The device name will be used as the licence name unless the application is for a family of medical devices. In this case, a generic licence name that covers all possible trade names (for example, urinary catheters) should be used.
help.licence.number.01: This number is assigned by Health Canada once a device(s) has been authorized for sale.
help.licence.number.02: This field will automatically add a leading zero if the Health Canada issued licence number is less than 6 digits.
help.licence.number.03: If the licence number has not yet been assigned, use 000000.
help.device.detail.01: The identifiers listed in the device details excel file must appear on and match the identifier listed on the submitted labelling.
help.device.detail.02: Required for all new licence applications, as well as any amendment applications that may impact a manufacturer's catalogue listing.
help.device.detail.03: The device details excel file is available on the REP Index page for medical devices (opens in a new window).
